food_subgroup: Fast food burger meal with fries and zero-calorie beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for a creamy mushroom double beef burger meal. FOODON_00002383 (hamburger food product) used as the closest defensible anchor for the burger component. Meal components (fries, zero-calorie beverage) are not individually coded here.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FAN (Hamburgers and similar products) is the closest defensible category for the burger component of this meal. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for a composite fast-food upsized meal.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this composite fast-food meal product.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (double burger + large fries + Coke Zero)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of one Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe Burger (approximately 280 g), one large portion of french fries (approximately 154 g), and one Coke Zero (approximately 316 g / 330 mL), totalling approximately 750 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe Burger, Large Fries, Coke Zero; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for beef burger patty, fast-food french fries, and cola zero-calorie beverage
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore (primary menu nutrition panel) / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (secondary cross-reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-serving nutrition for individual menu items. The upsized meal is a composite of: (1) Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe Burger, (2) Large Fries, and (3) Coke Zero. Per-100g values are derived by summing the published per-serving values for all three components and dividing by total meal weight (approximately 750 g). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries (USDA #21108 fast-food hamburger double patty with condiments; USDA #21138 fast-food potato french fried; USDA #14400 carbonated beverage cola zero calorie) were used to cross-check macro plausibility. Coke Zero contributes negligible macros. Values represent a weighted composite per 100 g of the full meal.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; GI of white bread bun component ~70–75; GI of french fries ~63; composite meal GI estimated at ~65 (medium) weighted by carbohydrate contribution of burger bun and fries
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe Upsized Meal as a composite product. GI estimated from peer-reviewed international GI tables (Atkinson et al. 2021, AJCN): white bread/burger bun GI ~70–75; french fries GI ~63 (Atkinson et al. 2021). Coke Zero contributes negligible carbohydrates and is excluded from GI weighting. Weighted composite GI calculated at approximately 65 (medium category). Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 163.5 g carbohydrate in full 750 g meal) / 100 ≈ 31.8. Carbohydrate per serving derived from per-100g value × 7.5 (750 g serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; approximately 2,850 mg sodium per full 750 g meal)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal, as a single serving provides approximately 2,850 mg sodium — exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg — and the high saturated fat content further elevates cardiovascular risk associated with elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~31.8 per meal) driven by refined carbohydrates in burger bun and large french fries
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal or consume it only occasionally with careful carbohydrate budgeting, as the high glycemic load of approximately 31.8 per serving — from the refined wheat bun and deep-fried potato fries — is likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; the high caloric density also contributes to weight management challenges central to glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~3.4 g per 100 g; ~25.5 g per meal), trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g), high sodium, and high caloric density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal due to its high saturated fat content (approximately 25.5 g per serving, exceeding the AHA recommended daily limit of 13 g), the presence of trans fat, and very high sodium levels, all of which are independently associated with increased risk of adverse cardiovascular events including myocardial infarction and stroke.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe Upsized Meal is a McDonald's Singapore limited or seasonal menu offering consisting of three components: a Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe Burger, a large portion of french fries, and a Coke Zero. The burger features two grilled beef patties topped with a rich creamy mushroom sauce, sautéed mushrooms, cheese, and lettuce, all served in a toasted wheat bun. The large fries are deep-fried potato strips seasoned with salt. Coke Zero is a zero-calorie carbonated cola beverage sweetened with artificial sweeteners. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and halal-certified at participating McDonald's Singapore outlets. Nutritionally, the full meal (~750 g) delivers approximately 1,485 kcal, 63.8 g protein, 69 g fat (including ~25.5 g saturated fat and ~1.5 g trans fat), 163.5 g carbohydrate, and approximately 2,850 mg sodium. The glycemic index of the meal is estimated at 65 (medium), driven primarily by the refined wheat bun and deep-fried potato fries, yielding a high glycemic load of approximately 31.8 per serving. This meal is of high clinical concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its very high sodium, saturated fat, trans fat, and glycemic load. It is not suitable for regular consumption in any of these clinical populations. The meal is sold at McDonald's Singapore outlets including Springleaf Tower and Boat Quay locations, and is listed on field survey data for delivery.

confidence_reason: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-serving nutrition information for individual menu items on its official website, which is the primary source used to derive composite per-100g values for this upsized meal. However, the exact per-serving weights of each meal component (burger, fries, beverage) required estimation based on standard McDonald's Singapore serving sizes, and the per-100g values are therefore adapted rather than directly published. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries were used for cross-validation of macro plausibility. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed international GI tables (Atkinson et al. 2021, AJCN) rather than a direct measurement of this specific product. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. This is a composite fast-food meal record. Per-100g nutrient values are derived from the weighted sum of three components: Creamy Mushroom Double Deluxe Burger (~280 g), Large Fries (~154 g), and Coke Zero (~316 g / 330 mL), totalling ~750 g. 2. Coke Zero contributes negligible macronutrients (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g are effectively zero for the beverage component); its contribution is naturally zero by product formulation (zero-calorie beverage). The beverage's zero macro contribution is absorbed into the composite per-100g calculation and does not require individual zero-macro justification as the composite meal macros are all non-zero. 3. Derived composite per-100g nutrient values from McDonald's Singapore official nutrition panel for individual meal components, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy.
Added multilingual local_names and aliases for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per G6 Singaporean dish requirements.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases consistency — all entries match verbatim.
